summary: I found one candidate P1 blocker in the external-review failure-evidence hardening: provider non-zero exits write reviewer invocation metadata without the new failure marker required to make missing normalized output schema-valid. I did not find a concrete blocker in the repeated review_control_rules/review_cycle policy; it remains duplication/backlog unless validation later finds contradictory behavior. I also noted a non-blocking stale self-application binding risk.

findings.title: Non-zero provider exits still produce schema-invalid failed invocation metadata
evidence: schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json:20-37 requires normalized_output_path but no longer requires normalized_output_hash at the top level.
schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json:217-226 requires failure_message when failure_stage is present, otherwise requires normalized_output_hash.
scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py:937-953 writes invocation metadata for exit_code != 0 without failure_stage, failure_message, or normalized_output_hash, then raises.

scripts/reviewers/run_external_reviewer.py:1003-1015 only adds failure_stage/failure_message if invocation_path does not already exist, so the non-zero exit metadata written at lines 951-953 is not repaired.
scripts/repo_validation/review.py:1143-1148 validates invocation metadata against the schema and only treats it as failed evidence when failure_stage is present.
run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/evidence-report.md:37-44 claims failed external reviewer invocation metadata is now schema-valid evidence.
findings.why_it_matters: The change claims to harden failed external-review evidence, but the actual provider failure path cannot satisfy the updated invocation schema. This recreates the prior failure mode where a live Claude review failure cannot be represented as durable schema-valid failure evidence.
findings.recommendation: Add failure_stage and failure_message before writing invocation metadata for exit_code != 0, and add a targeted test for a non-zero provider process result validating the invocation metadata against schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json.
findings.blocker_path: pr-merge-readiness requires failed external reviewer invocations to be recorded as non-completed but schema-valid evidence; the schema requires either normalized_output_hash or failure_stage, while the non-zero provider path writes neither.
findings.acceptance_impact: Accepting this branch would leave the advertised failure-evidence hardening broken for live provider non-zero exits, so a provider-mirrored review failure can still block or confuse readiness without valid failure evidence.
findings.calibration_reason: This is P1 because it is a concrete validator/schema contradiction on a failure path explicitly named by the rerun scope, not a style or duplication concern.

findings.title: Active pr-merge-readiness binding still points at the superseded material change
evidence: .agentsflow/workflows/pr-merge-readiness.binding.yaml:49 records raw_root under the older 2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness run.
run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/verification-gate-report.md:5-11 cites the same binding while declaring target content head 8a2c197.
findings.why_it_matters: The run artifacts consistently target 8a2c197, but the active project binding still carries fc5f09c-era self-application and raw evidence paths. That can confuse future reruns or operators about which run/material change the overlay is bound to.
findings.recommendation: Either update the binding to the rerun material change or remove run-specific fields from the reusable binding and keep material_change_id/run roots only in run artifacts.
findings.no_blocker_path_reason: I am not marking this P1 because the current review packet and verification artifacts are run-scoped to 8a2c197 and no final merge-ready claim has been made yet.

findings.title: Repeated review policy is duplication, not an acceptance blocker on current evidence
evidence: run-artifacts/agentsflow/runs/2026-06-23-v0.2-pr-merge-readiness-rerun/readiness-intake.md:45-48 classifies repeated review-control policy as non-blocking unless there is a concrete contradiction, broken validator path, or false readiness claim.

workflows/pr-merge-readiness/workflow.yaml:250-313 repeats review control and review cycle policy but keeps read-only reviewers, no hardcoded max_review_cycles, mandatory-evidence blocking, and no-majority-override semantics.
docs/review-agent-interaction-protocol.md:292-303 matches the two-control-reviewer collision rule and optional max_review_cycles policy.

docs/review-fusion-model.md:124-155 says workflows should not duplicate the shared finding-control procedure locally, but the checked pr-merge-readiness copy did not show a contradictory acceptance path.
findings.why_it_matters: Duplication raises maintenance risk, but I did not find a concrete current contradiction or false readiness path from this repetition.
findings.recommendation: Track factoring into a reusable policy/profile artifact after v0.2 unless another reviewer validates a concrete break path.
findings.no_blocker_path_reason: No grounded acceptance blocker found for the repeated review_control_rules/review_cycle text itself.

requests_for_additional_verification.request: Add a targeted wrapper test for a real provider non-zero exit path and validate the produced reviewer-invocation metadata against schemas/reviewer-invocation.schema.json.

self_declared_limitations: Read-only review only; I did not run tests, execute scripts, modify files, create patches, or update evidence.
I inspected the assigned packet, referenced artifacts, source files, schemas, validators, and git diff context, but did not invoke live Claude.
